rulings.Case Title: KYO-YA LTD., DBA THE PALACE HOTEL VS. FIRST HEALTH, AN ILLINOIS CORPORATION et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, MAY 25, 2007, LINE 11. PLAINTIFF KYO-YA L.T.D., D.B.A. THE PALACE HOTEL'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO SHIFT THE BURDEN OF PROOF UNDER AGUILAR BECAUSE PLAINTIFF HAS NOT DEMONSTRATED THAT A "LOCKOUT" WAS NOT A "STRIKE" OR A PART OF A STRIKE. ASSUMING PLAINTIFF SHIFTED THE BURDEN OF PROOF, DEFENDANT HAS PRESENTED EVIDENCE OF TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT: 5, 6, 8, 9, 10; ISSUE A: 11 THROUGH 14, 16 THROUGH 17, 19 THROUGH 20 AND 22 THROUGH 23; ISSUE B: 29; ISSUE C; 32, 34, 35, 37, 39 THROUGH 44. THE EVIDENTIARY RULINGS WILL BE SET FORTH ON THE RECORD AT THE HEARING BUT MANY OF PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTIONS ARE OVERRULED UNDER P.G. AND E. VERSUS G.W. THOMAS DRAYAGE AND RIGGING COMPANY (1968) 69 C.3D 33, 41 ALLOWING EXTRINSIC EVIDENCE TO INTERPRET A WRITTEN INSTRUMENT WHERE THE INTERPRETATION IS REASONABLE UNDER THE CIRCUMSTANCES. =(302/PJM/KD)
